summary: Imgur images in chat load again instead of showing broken placeholders.

bugFixes: Imgur links: direct i.imgur.com images now display in chat. Imgur was blocking hotlinked images from CyTube; the theme now fixes that automatically when messages appear.

highlights: Paste an Imgur image link in chat and see the picture load reliably.
Works for existing messages in the scrollback, not only new posts.
No extra steps for viewers — mods just need the channel on the latest theme build.

summary: Paste IMDB, Imgur, and Lensdump links in chat and see rich previews instead of bare URLs.

features: IMDB links: movie and person pages show a poster card with title, year, rating, and summary — same style as Letterboxd and TMDB cards.
Imgur and Lensdump: gallery, album, and direct image links embed inline like GIFs from Klipy or Tenor.
improvements: Channel mods: use Import Required BillTube Chat Filters in Channel Options to pick up the new link rules.
bugFixes: Imgur gallery links no longer leave stray tracking text after the embedded image.

highlights: IMDB title and name links expand into formatted poster cards in chat.
Imgur gallery and album links show the image inline.
Lensdump image and album links show the image inline.
Direct Imgur and Lensdump CDN links embed without extra clicks.
Mods: re-import BillTube chat filters once to enable the new rules.

summary: Chat commands are trimmed to essentials, and username clicks resolve more reliably across the channel page.

improvements: Userlist lookups: clicking a chat name or loading avatars uses the same matching logic everywhere, so mods and viewers see fewer missed profile menus.
Commands menu: the bogus !/me entry is gone — use /me (no exclamation mark) for action messages, as provided by CyTube.
deprecations: Chat commands: removed !bump, !next, !now, !pick, !roll, !dice, and !ask. Use CyTube's queue and skip tools, or type !help for the commands that remain.

highlights: Clicking usernames in chat opens the correct user menu more reliably.
Removed !bump, !next, and !now — use the playlist panel or CyTube queue controls instead.
Removed random commands !pick, !roll, !dice, and !ask.
Commands help (?) lists only commands that actually work.
Use /me for action messages — not !/me.

summary: Channel staff get a clearer Theme tab, audio adds mono mix, and personal theme settings stay focused on your look.

features: Mono audio: mix stereo playback to mono from audio options — handy for uneven tracks or listening on one side.
Channel Theme tab: admins can manage channel branding, optional extra scripts and styles, and the movie info overlay from Channel Settings again.
improvements: Channel settings: colors and fonts stay in your personal Theme settings; the channel panel focuses on channel-wide branding and resources.
Audio controls: volume boost and enhancement live together in one audio panel.
deprecations: Retired unused player extras (billcast, picture-in-picture dock, ratings overlay, auto-captions, and video enhancement toggles) to keep the experience lean.

highlights: Channel admins: Theme tab is back in Channel Settings for branding, resources, and movie info.
Personal colors and fonts stay in Theme settings — not duplicated in channel admin.
Mono audio option in audio controls.
Audio boost and enhancement grouped in one panel.
Unused overlay features removed to simplify the player.

summary: Narrow screens give video and chat more room, with a floating Menu and dockable panels.

features: Panels menu: dock playlist, MOTD, and polls to a side rail and open them in a slide-out sheet instead of losing them from the layout.
Mobile Menu: on stacked layouts, a floating Menu button opens chip-style shortcuts for Request, Options, Channel Settings, and Theme; your avatar stays visible with its own account menu.
improvements: Responsive layout: video and chat share the viewport fairly on phones and tablets without overlapping the playlist or add-media bar.
Chat toolbar: larger touch targets on emotes, GIFs, and other chat buttons.
bugFixes: Movie request modal: fits within the viewport on narrow screens.
Theme menu: Theme Settings opens correctly from the mobile Menu.

highlights: Video and chat get priority on narrow screens without overlap.
Floating Menu with chip shortcuts replaces the cramped top navbar on phones.
Dock panels to a side rail and slide them out when needed.
Larger chat toolbar buttons are easier to tap on touch screens.
Movie Request and Theme Settings work reliably on narrow viewports.
